def: s.f. The business or craft of a cutler.
s.f. Sugar.
s.f. Name of a rāginī; a kind of tune or music.

ety: prob. through Syriac, fr. Gr. Σκαμμωνία
def: s.m. (f. ?) Scammony (dried mucillage from the root of Convolvulus scammonea).
s.m. Inhabitants, people, inmates (of a house), family.

ety: sak˚ = Prk. सक्क(इ) = S. शक्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. शक्
def: v.n. To be able, can (commonly annexed to the base of a verb, or to an inflected infinitive, to form potentials; e.g. khā-saknā, 'to be able to eat'; jāne-saknā, 'to be able to go').
wety: Inherited from Apabhramsa सक्कइ (sakkaï), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀇 (sakkaï), from Sanskrit श॒क्नोति॑ (śaknóti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćaknáwti, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱek- + *-néwti.

def: s.m. = سکنة saknat, q.v.
v.n. = saknā, q.v.
s.f. A rest, pause (esp. in reading the Qorʼān); the quiescence of a letter, or its being void of a vowel.

ety: S. सङ्कोचः, rt. कुच् with सम्
def: s.m. Regard, respect, esteem, &c. (see saṅkoć).
v.t. = saṅkoćnā, q.v.
adj. Enraged, wrathful, angry.
adj. Full of anger, enraged, angry, displeased.
s.m. = sakorā, q.v.
s.m. A small earthen cup.
s.f. dim. of sakorā, q.v.

ety: caus. of sakaṛnā or sikuṛnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To draw together, to contract, compress, to draw up or in, to brace up, to tighten; to crumple up, &c.; — v.n. = sikuṛnā, q.v.
s.m. = شکر śakr, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Bad, impure, polluted (food); — impure food (cf. S. sakaṭānna); unfried food (regarded as not proper to be eaten outside of the rasoʼī, q.v.).

ety: prob. = sakṛā, q.v.; or S. सङ्कट+कः; Prk. संखडओ or संकडओ
def: adj. (f. -ī), Contracted, narrow, strait, not roomy, small, tight.

r: śakra, śakr, lit., Pentaptera arjuna, Nerium antidysentericum, śakrā-śan, ˚ra, aś˚, Wrightia antidysenterica, śakra-bhū-bhavā
def: s.f. Colocynth, Cucumis coloquintida
subs: s.m. Name of a red insect, the coccinella; the lady-bird.

ety: perf. part. of sakaṛnā, &c.; Prk. संकड्ढओ; S. सङ्क्टष्ट+कः
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Drawn together, contracted, compressed, braced up, tightened, tight; circumscribed; acute (angle); gathered up; shrunk, shrivelled, wrinkled, withered.

ety: sakrā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता + इका
def: s.f. Contractedness, narrowness, straitenedness, want of room, smallness.
s.f. Fee charge for acceptance of a bill of exchange ( = sakārā, q.v.).

ety: sakrā, 'narrow,' q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.t. To contract, to straiten, to deprive of necessary room (cf. sakṛānā).
v.t. To cause (one) to accept (a bill), to have or get (a bill) accepted.
v.t. To draw together, to contract, to brace, to tighten, &c. (see sukaṛnā); — v.n. = sukaṛnā.
s.f. = saṅkrānt or saṅkrānti, q.v.
adj. & s.f. See saṅkrāntī.
adv. Once; at once, together; together with, with; always, ever.
adj. Angry, wrathful, enraged.
(sa+ak˚), Having eyes; — having the seeds of which rosaries are made; having a rosary.
s.m. contrac. of sākshya, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Having syllables or letters, containing syllables; — s.m. Orthography.
adj. Orthographical.

adv. Before the eyes, in sight (of), in the presence (of), before; eye to eye, face to face; visibly, evidently, openly, manifestly; as, like, like as; — adj. Visible, evident, open, manifest, conspicuous; precisely like, exact, true, identical, the very same; — s.m. Interview, meeting
subs: adj. Causing to be visibly present, making evident, proving.

def: adj. & s.m. Observing, witnessing; attesting, testifying, bearing testimony, giving evidence; — observer, eye-witness, witness (in law)
subs: or sākshī karnā (-ko), To give (one) as a witness, to make (one) a witness, to call (one) to witness.

r: sākshī, sākshya, sākshī denā, sākshī karnā
def: s.f. = S ساکشيه साक्ष्य sākshya, s.m. Testimony, evidence, witness, attestation

r: sākshya, sākshī, sākshī denā, sākshī karnā
def: s.m. = H ساکشي साक्षी sākshī, s.f. Testimony, evidence, witness, attestation
adj. = شکت śakt, q.v.
s.m. Sparks struck from a steel; fire.
s.f. = شکتی śakti, q.v.
s.m. corr. of شاکت śākt, q.v.
s.m. A worshipper of the divine power under its female representation (as Devī, Durgā, &c.; see śakti).
adj. (f. -ā), Able, capable, competent; strong, mighty, powerful; hard, compact, tight, fast; clever, diligent; attentive, intent; affable, pleasing, civil, polite.

ety: for A. سکتة saktat, v.n. fr. سکت 'to be or become silent'
def: s.m. Apoplexy; a trance, swoon; stupefaction; — a pause, division, vacancy, hiatus.
s.f. Ability, capacity, capability, power, strength, hardness.
s.m. The thigh; a bone; — pole or shafts of a cart; frame of a cart.
s.f. Attachment, contact, union, junction, cohesion; addiction (to); devotion (to).

s.f. Ability, power, capacity, capability, faculty, strength, might, main, energy, ascendancy, prowess; the energy or active power of a deity personified as his wife (as Gaurī of Śiva, Lakshmī of Vishṇu, &c.); the female organ (as the counterpart of the phallic representation of Śiva, and worshipped either literally or figuratively by a sect of Hindūs termed Sāktas)
subs: śakti-mān, or śakti-mant, adj. (f. -matī), Possessed of ability, powerful, strong, mighty, able; possessing a competence

def: s.f. = śakti, and sakat, qq.v.
s.m. A casualty-list of horses that have died in the cavalry.
s.m. A species of poison.

r: saktu-phalā, lit., saktu, sattū, Mimosa suma
def: s.f. lit. 'Having fruit resembling meal' (saktu, vulg. sattū); the tree Mimosa suma.
s.m. = sāgūn, and sāl, qq.v.
s.f. Shrivelling up, contraction, compression; shrinking, shyness, coyness, bashfulness, shame; fear, dread, awe

ety: S. सङ्कुचित+कः, or perf. part. of sakućnā
def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Contracted, narrowed, compressed, tightened, tight; — shrivelled up, shrunk; closed, bound; abashed, ashamed; intimidated, awe-stricken ( = sakućāyā).
s.f. Shrinking; bashfulness; apprehensiveness; &c. (see sakuć).
v.t. To cause to shrink; to compress, to tighten; to abash; to frighten intimidate, overawe; — v.n. = sakućnā, q.v.
adj. = sakućāyā; — s.f. = sakućāʼī or sakuć.
part. adj. (f. sakućāʼī) = sakućā, q.v.
v.n. To shrink, contract, become narrow or tight; to shrink (from), be afraid (of), be apprehensive (of), to fear; to hesitate; to be abashed, be shy, or bashful.
adj. & s.m. Having a family; belonging to a noble family, of good lineage or stock; belonging to the same family; — a family man; — a kinsman; — a kind of fish (cf. saur).
s.m. A kind of fish (cf. saur)
subs: (˚la+ar˚), s.m. A species of the gilt-head, Sparus emarginatus
s.m. A kind of fish, the gilt-head.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شَکْل (šakl), borrowed from Arabic شَكْل (šakl).

def: s.m. corr. of شاقول shāqūl, q.v.
s.m. One of the same family and name ( = sa-gotra); a distant relation; a kinsman.
s.m. An omen ( = sagun, shugūn).
s.m. A bird (in general); a kite; an eagle; a vulture; — an auspicious object or mark, a lucky omen, a prognostic, an omen (in general, but rarely applied to anything inauspicious).
s.m. One who understands omens.
s.m. A bird (in general); the Indian kite, Falco cheela; — name of one of the astronomical periods called invariable karaṇs; — name of the Aśvins.
s.m. One who understands omens; — s.f. A hen-sparrow.
s.m. A bird.
s.m. A bird; a blue jay; the Indian vulture.
s.m. A bird; a blue jay; the Indian vulture.
s.m. Name of a famous Sanskrit drama by Kālidās.
s.m. = śakunt, q.v.
adj. Silent, quiet.

ety: intens. n. fr. سکت; see sākit
def: adj. Constantly or continually silent; — s.m. A silent man.
s.m. Name of the family, par excellence, of the Buddha (founder of the Buddhist religion); — name of Buddha himself
subs: s.m. 'The Śākya saint'; and śākya-siṅh, s.m. 'The Śākya lion'; epithets of Buddha.

def: adj. Possible, capable, practicable, capable of being effected or done, easy to be accomplished; requiring to be effected or done; that may be conveyed or expressed (as sense or meaning by any particular word); — s.m. (in Gram.) The meaning of a word.
s.f. Possibility, practicability, capability.

s.m. The saul tree, Shorea robusta, and its wood; — the plane-tree; — a wall surrounding a building, a wall (in general); enclosure, court; — a kind of fish, Ophiocephalus wrahl; — the gilt-head fish, Sparus spilotus
subs: s.f. or sāl-pāṇi, s.f. lit. 'Having sāl leaves'; the shrub Hedysarum gangeticum
s.m. A flower of the sāl-tree; — Hibiscus mutabilis
s.m. The resinous juice of the sāl-tree.

def: s.f. House, place, hall; school-house.
s.m. A thorn; — (fig.) pain, affliction, trouble; — perforation, bore, orifice, mortise; a hole made by driving a pin into the ground, &c.
s.m. A jackal ( = siyār, q.v.).

ety: Pehl. śāl; Zend śaredha; S. शरद्
def: s.m. A year
subs: adv. A whole year; all the year round
s.m. & adv. The year before last
s.m. or sāl-tamāmī, s.f. An annual report
adv. At the end of the year
Yearly account of receipts and disbursements; debit and credit account
(or ḥisābī sāl), s.m. Official or financial year
adj. Old in years, old, aged; worn out; — experienced
s.m. Intercalary year; leap year; a year of thirteen months, which comes round every three years

s.f. lit. 'Year-knot, or age-knot'; birthday, anniversary of (one's) birthday (when a knot is tied in a string or thread kept as a record of one's age;-syn. baras-gāṅṭh):-sāl-ě-guẓashta, s.m. The past year, last year
s.m. The mercantile year (concurrent with the Samvat year, but commencing two months later)
adv. By the year; according to the year or years
adv. lit. 'Years and months'; always, at all times.

def: s.m. (dialec.) The large green grasshopper; the mantis or leaf-insect.
s.m. Stake, spike, splinter, thorn; difficulty, embarrassment, &c. (see śalya)
subs: (apnā), To meet or manage a difficulty, &c., to manage difficult affairs (of a house, &c.); to prepare (one's own) funeral pyre, to become a satī
v.n. To be clear or free of difficulty or embarrassment, to be settled or composed.

def: adj. (colloq.) One hundred ( = sau).
s.m. Water ( = sara or sar).
s.m. The tree Shorea robusta ( = sāl); — a kind of fish, Ophiocephalus wrahl; — a name of king Śāli-vāhan
subs: or śāl-bhanjī, s.f. A doll, a wooden puppet; a kind of game played in the east of India
s.f. lit. 'Having śāl leaves'; the shrub Hedysarum gangeticum
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شال (šāl). Doublet of سَاڑِی (sāṛī). Compare Punjabi ਸ਼ਾਲ (śāl) / شال (śāl), Gujarati શાલ (śāl), Marathi शाल (śāl), Kannada ಶಾಲು (śālu), Telugu శాలువ (śāluva).

def: s.m. A porcupine's quill; — a dart, spear: — a kind of field; — an epithet of Brahmā.
s.f. House, place (used chiefly in comp., e.g. dharm-sālā, pāṭ-sālā, qq.v., and cf. sāl).
s.m. Wife's brother, brother-in-law; a term of abuse.
s.f. A large branch of a tree; — a hall, room, house, place; a school, a stable ( = sālā).
s.f. Calling or inviting (beggars, &c.), to receive or partake of food; invitation; annunciation; proclamation; voice, call, cry (of an auctioneer, or salesman, &c.); shout, challenge (of a foe).
(fr. sāl), adj. aff. Years old, &c. (used with numerals, e.g. du-sāla, 'Two years old'; ṣad-sāla, 'of a hundred years').
s.m. A fox
subs: s.f. Salep, the root of a species of orchis, Orchis mascula (considered, particularly in the East, to be a strong restorative and aphrodisiac).

def: s.m. A (hard, rough) stone or slab for grinding drugs, &c. on (syn. kharal).

ety: for A. صلابة, inf. n. of صلب 'to be hard,' &c.
def: s.f. Firmness, hardness, stiffness, rigidity; severity, rigour; strength; valour; majesty, dignity, awe.
s.m. = شلبهہ śalabh, q.v.
s.m. A grasshopper; a locust.

ety: inf. n. of سلف 'to pass away,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Past, preceding, former (times, &c.); — such as have gone before, predecessors, ancestors; past or former times; — money advanced for merchandise, &c.; — a purse, bag.
adj. Joined together, in one piece, unbroken, continuous, uninterrupted; — s.m. (local), Close friendship, intimacy, familiarity.

s.m. A species of black quartzose (found in the river Gandhak), containing the impression of one or more ammonites (conceived by the Hindūs to represent Vishṇu; and hence it is worshipped by the Vaishṇavas).

ety: inf. n. of صلح 'to be good, right, just, &c.
def: s.f. Goodness (of character, or of state or condition); well-being, prosperity; rectitude, virtue, probity, honesty; right or proper course (of conduct or procedure); good and proper thing, right thing, prudent measure; advisableness, propriety, fitness; advice, counsel; consultation; — (quasi-inf. n. of اصلح, iv of صلح), rectification, adjustment, agreement, harmony, concord, reconciliation, peace, treaty ( = iṣlāḥ, q.v.); — adj. Good, right, proper, suitable, favourable, advisable, prudent
subs: (kisi-kī), To act according to the advice (of)
(-ko), To give advice (to), to advise, counsel
(-se), To hold consultation (with), to advise (with), to consult
(-kī), To take the advice (of); to consult
Is not advisable; is inexpedient; is not a prudent course
s.f. A fit or proper opportunity, a favourable time; — the right course for the time or occasion; — adj. Advisable.

ety: for A. صلاحية, inf. n. of صلح 'to be good,' &c.
def: s.f. Goodness, rectitude, integrity, probity, honesty; virtue, chastity; sanctity; — welfare, well-being, prosperity; — mildness, gentleness; — ability, capability, fitness, worth; — a deposition; a report or return (made by the police) on the state of a district
subs: s.f. A diary (kept in police and revenue offices) of the condition or prosperity of a district
(-kī), To make a (police) report on the state of a district; to keep a register of travellers in a sarāʼc, &c.

def: s.f. A piece of wire, a needle, a probe, any pointed surgical instrument; a catheter; a bodkin; a large needle used for tinging the eyes with collyrium; a piece of wire, or needle, &c. used to deprive a person of sight (by drawing it, while hot, over the eyes); a lead-pencil; a brimstone match; a line, streak, stripe
subs: (-par), To pass a red-hot wire or needle, &c. (across the eyes of a person), to deprive of sight, to blind

def: s.f. Brother-in-law's wife ( = sarhaj).
s.f. = sarhaj and salaij, qq.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Feeling shame or modesty, bashful, modest, ashamed.
adj. Painful, afflictive, troublous.
s.f. (local) = salāḵẖ, q.v.; and cf. next.
s.f. A probe; a rule, ruler, &c. (see śalākā); a line, streak.

s.f. A dart, a javelin; an arrow; a rib (of an umbrella, &c.); a thin bar (of a cage, &c.); a small stake, stick, rod, peg, pin; a round pointed piece of wood or bamboo (serving for various purposes); a rule, ruler; a brimstone-match; any pointed surgical instrument, a tent or probe; a bone; an oblong quadrangular piece of ivory or bone (used in particular games), a domino; a fibrous stick (used as a brush, &c.); a tooth-brush; a toothpick; a pencil; — a porcupine; — a kind of bird, Turdus salica.
s.f. A bar (of iron); an ingot (of gold or silver); a stake; a spit; a probe; — a continuous line, a streak.
v.t. To rule lines; to mark with lines, to streak.
s.m. = S شللي शलली śalalī, s.f. A porcupine's quill.
s.f. = S شلل शलल śalal, s.m. A porcupine's quill.

ety: inf. n. of سلم 'to be or become safe,' &c.
def: s.m. Safety, peace; salutation, greeting, compliments; parting salutation, adieu, farewell, good-bye
subs: To look to the right and the left after prayer
(-ko), To bid adieu (to), to dismiss; (among Europeans) to present (one's) compliments (to); to request the presence (of a person); to desire permission to enter
Peace be with you; good morning to you
(-ko), To salute, greet, hail, accost; to bid adieu (to), say farewell (to); to give up, to desist or refrain (from); to bow (to), to acknowledge the superiority of
(-kā), To accept and return the salutation (of)
ex: Send my regards to your father.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَام (salām), itself from Arabic سَلَام (salām). First attested in c. 1503 as Middle Hindi سلام (slam).

ety: inf. n. of سلم 'to be safe,' &c.
def: s.m. Saluting; submitting; cultivating peace; friendship; — peace, safety; — adj. Free from fault or flaw, sound; — sallamahuʼl-lāhǒ taʻālā, May the Most High God save or protect him.

r: ṣalʻam, ṣallaʼllāh ʻalaihi wa sallam, ṣallạ̄
def: a contrac. (often added to the name of Mohammad), formed from the abbreviation of the words ṣallaʼllāh ʻalaihi wa sallam, 'May God bless him and grant him peace' (see صلی ṣallạ̄).

ety: for A. سلامة, inf. n. of سلم; see salām
def: s.f. Safety, salvation; tranquillity, peace, rest, repose; immunity; liberty; soundness; recovery; health; — adj. & adv. (used predicatively) Safe, sound, well; — in safety, safely, securely
subs: s.m. An economist; a good manager or administrator; a well-conducted person
or rauʼī, s.f. Economy, good management; moderation; good behaviour
v.n. To be or remain safe and sound, to live long, to prosper.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَلَامَت (salāmat), from Arabic سَلَامَة (salāma).

def: s.f. Safety, security, protection; peace, tranquillity; health; good condition, well-being; the being alive and in health; — a kind of coarse cloth; — adv. In peace or safety; in health
subs: (-kī), To drink to the health (of), to quaff a cup to the health (of).

ety: salām+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Salutation; salute (as by presentation of arms, &c.), salute of cannon; — a present on being introduced to a superior; a present given to a landlord on granting a lease, &c.; a present (on particular occasions) to a person making a salute; a congratulatory present to a bridegroom (generally from the father-in-law's household; — cf. salām-karāʼī); — slope, incline; a pavement across the bed of a watercourse
subs: s.f. A present from a farmer to his landlord to enable him to build his house; a compulsory present exacted by the landlord from a tenant on his erecting a new hut.

def: s.m. A curry of meat, fish, or vegetables.
wety: Uncertain. Some theories: * Inherited from Sanskrit *संलेपन (*saṃlepana, “smearing together”), from सम्- (sam-) + लिप् (lip, “to smear”) + -न (-na). * Inherited from Sanskrit *संलापन (*saṃlāpana), from the root ली (lī, “to attach”). * Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀮𑁄𑀡 (saloṇa), 𑀲𑀮𑀽𑀡 (salūṇa), from Sanskrit सलवण (salavaṇa, “having salt”), from स- (sa-) + लवण (lavaṇa). Compare सलोना (salonā, “tasty”).

ety: sāl, q.v.+Zend aff. āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & adv. Yearly, annual; annually, per year; — s.m. Annual pension; annuity; — land taken up for the whole year
adj. Penetrable.
s.f. coutrac. of salūno, q.v.

ety: sāl, q.v.+ār = āwar = prep. ā+war = Zend ā+bara = S. आ+भर
def: s.m. Leader in war, general or commander-in-chief, a title given by Eastern princes to their nobles
subs: s.m. Chief of a caravan, leader of a troop
s.m. Head of a clan or tribe.

def: s.m. A ladder; a flight of stairs or steps; — a bird-cage; — the claw of an elephant.
adj. (prop. fem. of s̤alās̤at), Three.

ety: for A. سلاسة, inf. n. of سلس 'to be easy,' &c.
def: s.f. Easiness, simplicity; plainness, clearness, perspicuity; facility; ductility; gentleness, tractableness; urbanity.
s.f. pl. (of silsilat), Chains.

ety: quasi-inf. n. of صلّي 'to pray,' ii of صلو
def: s.f. Prayer, supplication (esp. one of the five stated prayers enjoined on good Mohammadans in the course of the twenty-four hours); — invocation of (God's) blessing (on), a blessing, benediction; — the Fātiḥa or opening chapter of the Qorʼān.

ety: quasi-inf. n. of صلّي 'to pray,' ii of صلو
def: s.f. Prayer, supplication (esp. one of the five stated prayers enjoined on good Mohammadans in the course of the twenty-four hours); — invocation of (God's) blessing (on), a blessing, benediction; — the Fātiḥa or opening chapter of the Qorʼān.
s.f. Prayer, &c. ( = صلات ṣalāt, q.v.); compassion or mercy (of God).
s.m. pl. (of sult̤ān), Emperors, kings, monarchs.
s.f. (ironic. & colloq.) Curses, maledictions; abuse, insult
s.f. Burning; — crucifying, crucifixion; — teasing, fretting, vexing.

ety: inf. n. of سلب 'to seize, carry off by force,' &c.
def: s.m. Seizing by force, spoiling, depriving of; deprivation; negation, denial, negative argument (in logic); — spoil, plunder, booty
subs: v.n. To be carried off; to be deprived of; to be wanting in, be devoid of; to be lost.

def: s.f. Vice (in a horse).
s.m. Sage, Salvia.
adj. = salonā, q.v.

ety: act. part. of صلح 'to be good, or just,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Good, right, just, righteous, virtuous, honest; sound, proper, fit, apt; — a good or virtuous man, a man of probity and honour; a sedate, steady person (opp. to enthusiast).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَالِح (sālih), from Arabic صَالِح (ṣāliḥ).

def: (for A. صالحة, fem. of ṣāliḥ), adj. f. Good, virtuous, chaste, &c.
s.m. The phlegmatic humour, phlegm, rheum, defluxion, catarrh (syn. zukām).
s.m. corr. of شلغم shalgam, q.v.
adv. For many years
subs: sālhāʼě-sāl, s.m. A course or series of years, several years; — adv. For years; year after year.

def: s.f. = salaij, q.v.
s.m. = شالي śāli, q.v.
s.f. Wife's sister (esp. younger sister), sister-in-law.
adj. (f. -inī), Belonging to a house, domestic; — (as last member of compounds) endowed with, possessed of, having; inclined or disposed to; shining, resplendent with.
adj. Annual, relating to the year; — s.f. Land taken by the year.
s.m. Rice; a kind of white rice (growing in deep water); red rice (requiring only a moist soil); — the civet-cat; pole-cat
subs: s.m. (f. -ī), lit. 'Receiving oblations of rice or corn'; a horse; name of a writer on veterinary subjects (cf. sālotrī, salotrī)

ety: v.n. fr. صلب 'to be hard'; 'to crucify'
def: adj. Hard, firm, rigid, stiff; hardy, strong; — three-cornered; cross-shaped; — s.m. A cross, crucifix

ety: for A. سالبة, fem. of sālib, act. part. of سلب 'to seize'
def: s.m. A negative proposition (in logic).
s.m. See s.v. شالي śāli.

ety: act. part. of سلك 'to go along, to travel,' &c.
def: part. & s.m. Going, travelling; — traveller; (met.) devotee; a class of Moḥammadans who observe the law and lead a domestic life; an individual of that class (P. plur. sālikān).
adj. Relating or belonging to the śāl tree; belonging to a hall, &c.; — s.m. A weaver; a village of artificers; — a toll, tax
subs: s.m. One of the main divisions of the Mahābhārat.

ety: for A. سليقة salīqat, v.n. fr. سلق 'to adapt or dispose to,' &c.
def: s.m. Nature, natural disposition or constitution; genius; taste; good disposition; method, knack, way; knowledge, skill, dexterity, address
subs: salīqa-shěʻār, salīqa-mand, and salīqa-wālā, adj. Of good disposition; well-conducted; possessed of genius; discerning, discreet
s.m. or salīqe-kī guftgū, s.f. Polite conversation

def: s.m. Water; liquid (in general).
adj. (f. -ā), Playful, sportive, wanton.

ety: act. part. of سلم 'to be safe,' &c.
def: adj. Safe, secure, free; sound, perfect; whole; (in Gram.) sound or regular (a verb or word, i.e. not having one of the weak letter ا, و, ي for one of its radical letters, or the second radical similar to the third).
adj. Safe, secure, free; sound, perfect; healthy; right; pacific, mild, meek, affable
subs: adj. & s.m. Right-minded; sound; affable, &c. ( = salīm)
a right-minded person; — a real genius

def: s.f. A kind of cloth.

ety: act. part. of ثلث 'to form a third,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Third; — a third person, a mediator, arbitrator, umpire
subs: s.m. An unprejudiced or impartial arbitrator
s.m. An award by arbitration or by a jury, a deed of award.

ety: prob. corr. of A. salis; or an unclassical form fr. A. rt. سلس
def: adj. Easy, simple, plain, not abstruse
subs: adj. & s.m. Writing a plain hand; — one who writes a plain hand.

ety: v.n. fr. سلس 'to be loose or slack,' &c.
def: adj. Easy, gentle, tractable, docile, mild, compliant, submissive; ductile.

ety: for A. ثالثة s̤ālis̤at, fem. of s̤ālis̤
def: adj. & s.f. Third; — mediatrix, arbitress.
s.f. Mediation, arbitration.
s.f. Easiness, simplicity, plainness, clearness.
s.m. A canvas sack (in which baggage, &c. is fastened previously to being loaded on cattle).
s.m. A canvas sack (in which baggage, &c. is fastened previously to being loaded on cattle).
s.f. A river, stream.
s.m. corr. of shalīta, q.v.
s.m. Name of a celebrated sovereign of India, institutor of the era now called śāka or sākā (his capital was Pratishṭhāna on the Godāvarī: he was the enemy of Vikramāditya).
s.m. Name of a celebrated ancient sovereign of India, whose capital was Pratishṭhān on the Godāvarī (he was the institutor of the era now called śāka, and the enemy of Vikramāditya).
adj. &c. = sālāna, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of سلخ 'to strip off,' &c.
def: s.f. The last or end (of a month), the last day (of a month of thirty days), the day of new moon.
s.f. The root of the lotus or water-lily.

ety: inf. n. of سلّ 'to draw out or forth'
def: s.m. Drawing out or forth (esp. a sword from the scabbard).
3rd. pers. sing. pret. (of form ii of صلو), He blessed; or (optatively) may he bless
subs: (or -him), May God bless (or confer blessing upon) him (or them).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَلَّیٰ (sallā), from Arabic صَلَّى (ṣallā, “he blessed; may he bless”).

def: s.m. The plant Bignonia indica; — bark, rind.
s.m. A skinner, flayer.
s.f. The gum olibanum tree, Boswellia thurifera.
s.f. A porcupine; — the gum olibanum tree, Boswellia thurifera
s.m. A kind of coarse cloth.
adj. & s.f. Foolish, silly (woman); — a silly woman.
s.m. A thong, a narrow strip of leather inserted in the seams of shoes, saddles, &c.; a latchet.
s.m. A band of embroidery
subs: s.m. A kind of embroidery consisting of small stars between embroidered bands.

def: s.f. See شالملي śālmali, and سيمل semal.
s.f. The silk-cotton tree, Bombax heptaphyllum ( = semal); — one of the seven Dvīpas or great divisions of the known continent (so called from the above tree, said to grow there).

ety: sāl˚ = Prk. सार(इ), or सारे(इ) = S. शारय(ति), caus. of rt. शृ; — or = Prk. साल(इ), &c., fr. the S. caus. of शल्
def: v.t. To penetrate, pierce, prick; to perforate, bore, drill; — v.n. To prick, smart, ache, pain.

ety: S. सर, or सल+nā = Prk. डओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A rivulet.
s.m. A curry of meat, fish, or vegetables.
v.n. To be perforated, be pierced; to pierce, prick, enter (into).
adj. Being in the same world; — s.m. Good or respectable people, gentlefolk.
s.f. Being in the same world or sphere (with).
adj. (f. -ā), Playful, sportive, wanton.
adj. Having salt, salted (food), salty; — name of a district.
adj. (f. -ī), Salt, salted; seasoned; tasteful, tasty, savoury; beautiful, handsome; interesting, piquant; intelligent; dark-complexioned, nut-brown (complexion); expressive (countenance).
s.m. A horse-doctor, veterinary surgeon; a farrier.
s.m. = sālotrī, q.v.
s.m. A horse-doctor, veterinary surgeon; a farrier.
s.m. Green barley (used as fodder).

ety: prob. fr. the Port.; — Sp. zarza; It. salsa
def: s.m. Sarsa or sarsaparilla.
s.m. Name of a fountain in Paradise; — running limpid water, pure, sweet water.

ety: salsalā(nā)+āhaṭ = āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. इतव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Creeping, crawling; sound as of a snake creeping, rustling; creeping sensation, titillation, irritation (caused by the crawling of an insect on the body), formication; itching; prurience; pricking, piercing; uneasy feeling, doubt; rankling (cf. sursurāhaṭ).

ety: prob. S. शल् redupl.+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi (caus. augment); cf. sarsarānā
def: v.n. To creep, crawl; to feel a creeping or tickling sensation; to itch; to prick, pierce; to be troubled with doubt; to be uneasy; to rankle (in); — to make a rustling sound (as the creeping of a snake), to rustle; — v.t. To cause to simmer or boil, &c., to heat.
s.f. contrac. of salsalāhaṭ, q.v.

ety: for A. سلطنة, v.n. fr. سلط; see sult̤ān
def: s.f. Power of dominion, sovereign or ruling power, empire, sovereignty, dominion, rule; — kingdom, empire, realm
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سلطنت (saltanat), from Arabic سَلْطَنَة (salṭana).

def: s.m. A kind of fine red cloth, Turkey red.
s.m. A child's bib; a pinafore; a couple of pinafores, one worn on the breast, the other on the back, and fastened above and below the shoulders.
s.f. The day of full moon in the month Sāwan (July-August), the festival of rakshā-ban-dhan (see rākhī).
s.m. A little, very little.
s.m. The Trinity
s.m. = P سالوسي sālūsī, s.f. Trick, fraud, deceit, subterfuge, hypocrisy.
s.f. = P سالوس sālus, s.m. Trick, fraud, deceit, subterfuge, hypocrisy.
(v.n. fr. سلو 'to be content,' &c.), s.m.(?) A quail; — honey.
s.f. The cost of having (a thing) perforated or pierced, &c.
v.t. To cause to be perforated or pierced, &c., to have or get (a thing) pierced, &c.
adj. See s.v. sāl, 'year.'

ety: for A. سلوة, inf. n. of سلو 'to be content,' &c.
def: s.f. Patient endurance, content, comfort, solace, cheerfulness, happiness, tranquillity.
s.m. = شليه śalya, q.v.

s.m. A dart, javelin, spear, pike; an arrow; an iron crow; a stake, rod, spike, splinter, thorn; a pin, peg; — a porcupine; a hedgehog; — extraction of splinters or extraneous substances in surgery; any extraneous substance lodged in the body (as an arrow, or a splinter, &c.); a bone; the thorny shrub Vangueria spinosa; — a fence, a boundary; — sin, crime; poison; difficulty, embarrassment, distress; defamation, abuse; — name of the king of Madra (maternal uncle of the Pāṇdav princes); name of a son of Vipraćitti
subs: s.m. That branch of surgery which treats of the removal of extraneous substances lodged in the body

(˚ya+ud˚), s.m. The extraction of extraneous substances (as arrows, splinters, thorns, &c.) from the body; the ceremony of cleansing the site of a new house from bones or other unclean substances; the ceremony of cleansing or purifying a new house.

ety: Pārsī śãm; Pehl. śām; Zend śāma, rt. śam = S. सम्
def: s.m. Name of a celebrated hero in Persian romance (father to Zāl, and grandfather to Rustam); — a swelling (e.g. sar-sām, 'swelling in the head').
s.f. A ferule ( = sāmī).
s.m. = syām; — 2˚, s.f. = shām, qq.v.
s.m. = شم śam, q.v.
insep. prep. or prefix = sam, q.v.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: adj. Black, dark ( = syām); — s.m. A name of Krishṇ
subs: s.m. 'Dark and beautiful'; an epithet of Krishṇ; a proper name.

def: s.f. The iron head of a pestle, &c.; ferule (of a stick, &c.), mounting; a socket; (in Mech.) a carrier
subs: (-meṅ), To tip or shoe with iron, &c.; to get mounted; to put on a ferule, &c.

def: s.m. Appeasing; reconciling; conciliation; reconciliation.
adv. Happily, fortunately, auspiciously.
s.m. Hearing, listening to; — music, &c. ( = samāʻ); — the sense of hearing; — the organ of hearing, the ear
subs: s.f. Grating or jarring on the ear; dazing the brains (by excessive talk, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سَمْع (sam'), from Arabic سَمْع (samʕ).

def: insep. prep. or prefix (opp. to vi, and, like Gr. σύν, Lat. con, expressing) With, together with, along with, together: (it sometimes intensifies the idea contained in the simple root, and may often be translated by) much, greatly, very, wholly, thoroughly, perfectly, well, beautifully, &c.
s.f. Apparatus, &c. ( = sāmagrī, and sāmān, qq.v.); — provisions, food of various kinds.
s.m. Time, season; season of plenty, season of greatest abundance or prevalence; plenty, abundance; opportunity; — state, condition; concord, harmony, unison; — scene, landscape, view
subs: (-kā), To make a picture or representation (of), to represent; — to act, sing, or play in concert, to produce harmony; to be in concert or harmony
v.n. To find or get an opportunity.

def: s.f. A year.

ety: v.n. fr. سمو 'to be or become high,' &c.
def: s.m. The heavens, sky, firmament; a canopy; height, altitude, meridian, highest or uppermost part (of anything), culminating point, prime, spring.

s.m. Hearing, listening to; listening to music or singing; the sense of hearing; — singing, song, music (instrumental or vocal); ecstasy occasioned by hearing singing or music (particularly in Derwishes when hearing hymns).
s.m. Blackness, impurity, mouldiness, &c.; — a kind of edible grain, Panicum frumentaceum or P. colonum.
s.f. (m. ?) A beautiful Indian song-bird, the Indian nightingale, Turdus macrourus.
s.m. A hymn, a particular kind of sacred text or verse that is to be chanted
subs: vulg. sām-bed, s.m. Name of one of the three principal Vedas (the prayers of which are composed in metre, and are always sung or chanted)
vulg. sām-bedī, s.m. A Brāhmaṇ who studies the Sāma-veda.

def: adj. Even, level, flat, plain, smooth; same, equal, like, similar; all, whole, entire, full, complete; — s.m. A level plain; (in Geom.) a mean proportional segment; — (in music) measuring time; time; a tone
subs: (˚ma+āć˚), adj. & s.m. Equal or similar in practice or in virtuous conduct; — equal or similar conduct; upright or virtuous conduct, proper practice
adj. & s.m. Of like nature or property; — equality, similarity, equability, sameness; equilibrium; goodness
s.f. Even or level ground; a race-course
adj. & s.m. Having three sides equal; — an equilateral triangle

adj. & adv. Of equal weight, equiponderant; equal, even, matching, rivaling, of the same grade, of equal ability, of equal value or merit; duly balanced, equable; evenly or harmoniously proportioned (as a temperament or constitution, &c.); — evenly, equally on both sides (as a weight pressing, &c.)
adj. (f. -ā), Of the same caste or class, of the same origin or kind, homogeneous
adj. & s.m. Equally four-sided; — an equilateral tetragon or rhombus
vulg. sam-darsī, adj. & s.m. Viewing or regarding equally, looking at both sides impartially, impartial; — an impartial person
adj. & s.m. Sharing (another's) pain or grief, feeling for another's woe, sympathizing (with); — a sympathetic person, sympathizer
adj. Having the same grief and joy, sympathizing in sorrow and joy
adj. Plane, level, level with the ground
adj. (f. -ā), Belonging to the same country or locality
adj. Of the same form or figure; — s.m. Conformity; uniformity
s.m. The horizon; the north or south point of the horizon; a pole of the prime vertical
s.m. Equality in wealth; — adj. (f. -ā), Possessing equal wealth
sam-kālin, or sam-kālīn, adj. Of or belonging to the same time or period, contemporary, contemporaneous, coeval
s.f. A suitable maiden, a girl fit to be married
adj. & s.m. Rectangular, right-angled; — a right angle
s.m. A species of metre in which all the four feet are equal

def: s.m. Quiet, tranquillity, rest, calm, peace; quietism, quiet of mind; restraint of the organs of sense; indifference to objects of sense; the absence of passion; indifference, stoicism; abstract meditation on Brahmā; emancipation from all the illusions of existence, final happiness; — allayment (of pain), alleviation or cure (of disease), convalescence; — the hand; — imprecation, malediction, abuse.
s.m. sāmak, and sāṅwā, qq.v.
s.f. corr. of شياما śyāmā, q.v.
adj. Roomy, capacious, fit to hold; — s.m. A vessel in which fire is kept.

ety: for A. سماعة, inf. n. of سمع 'to hear'
def: s.f. Hearing, the sense of hearing; hearing or taking evidence (in a law-suit, &c.), cognizance
subs: (-kī), To hear, to take evidence (in a suit), to try (a case), to entertain or take cognizance (of a complaint)
adj. Worthy or deserving of a hearing, admissible, cognizable (a suit).

def: s.m. See s.v. sama or sam.
s.m. (in (rhetoric) Mentioning together; proximity (to), or association (with a word the meaning of which is well understood); bringing together, association, company.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. sama or sam.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit समाचार (samācāra, “proceeding, conduct, news, information”).

def: s.m. Proceeding, going; performance, practice, conduct, behaviour; usage, way; proper practice or behaviour; state, condition, circumstances, doings, news, report, information, tidings, intelligence, message, advice (of a bill), account of circumstances, health; an occurrence; tradition
subs: (-kā), To give news or tidings (of), to report
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit समाचार (samācāra, “proceeding, conduct, news, information”).

ety: v.n. fr. صمد 'to repair, or have recourse (to
def: s.m. A lord, chief man, master, head (of a family, &c.); the Most High, the Eternal, God; — adj. High, sublime; perpetual, eternal.
s.m. corr. of sam-vād, q.v.
adj. Eternal; divine ( = ṣamadī; for the -ānī, which is borrowed from the Aramaic, cf. jismānī, rūḥānī, &c.).
s.m. Great respect, reverence, veneration.
adj. See s.v. sama or sam.

s.f. Profound or abstract meditation, restraining the senses and confining the mind to contemplation (especially on the true nature of spirit, &c.); intense absorption or contemplation; silence, devotion; abstracted or absorbed state; a religious vow or self-imposed restraint; self-immolation (of a sanyāsī) by drowning or by burying himself alive; an exercise of austerity (among jogīs) whereby they are supposed to acquire the power of suspending during pleasure the connexion between soul and body; — the tomb of a sanyāsī (who has been buried alive); a tomb over the relics of a dead body, or a little edifice, to contain the tulsī plant, which is erected over the burial place of a sanyāsī
subs: samādh lenā, To suspend (one's) breath for a fabulous period of time; to submit to be buried alive.

def: s.m. The mutual embracing of two samdhīs.

s.m. Fixing the mind in abstract contemplation (as on the true nature of spirit), religious abstraction (as practised by Hindūs), restraining the senses by confining the mind to contemplation, profound absorption or contemplation, religious meditation; intentness, eagerness; — promising; declaring, declaration; adjustment, decision, determination; the act of satisfying, propitiating; pacification; comfort, consolation, solace.

s.m. Putting together; collecting or composing the mind, fixing the thoughts; contemplation, profound or abstract meditation, &c. (see samādh); composing or reconciling of differences; the laying up a store of grain; agreement, promise, engagement, vow; religious vow or obligation; requital; — a burial; tomb, grave; the joint of the neck; the attempting impossibilities; demonstrated conclusion
subs: s.m. The place where a sanyāsī has been buried alive; buring place, cemetery

def: adj. & s.m. Absorbed in contemplation, meditative; — a meditative or thoughtful person.
s.m. See s.v. sam.
s.m. Overseer, superintendent.

s.m. Coming together, encounter, meeting, union, junction, coalition, assembling; association, intercourse, acquaintance, society, company; an assembly; — arrival, approach; — similar condition or progress
part. Come together, met, encountered, joined, united; assembled; in conjunction; come to, approached arrived.
s.f. Coming together encountering; meeting; assembly; union joining; approach arrival.
s.f. Coming together encountering; meeting; assembly; union joining; approach arrival.
adj. All, entire, whole, full, complete.
adj. All, entire, whole, full, complete.

s.f. Collection of materials or implements, &c.; stock, effects, furniture, baggage, goods, chattels, articles, things, materials, necessaries, utensils, implements, apparatus, tools; train, retinue; pomp, circumstance (syn. sāmān); scenery; — entireness, wholeness, totality, the whole, completeness, perfection ( = S. sāmagrya).

ety: for A. سماحة, inf. n. of سمح 'to be liberal,' &c.
def: s.f. Liberality, bereficence, bountifulness.
s.m. Setting animals to fight for sport, matching animals to fight for a wager.
adj. What has been heard or heard of, what has been received by hearsay, traditional; (in Gram.) irregular.
s.f. The act of containing or holding (anything); room, space, capacity; capability, competency, ability (syn. gunjāʼish).
s.f. Endurance, patience, forbearance, self-control.
s.f. (colloq.) = samā or samay, q.v.
s.m. = samay, q.v.

s.m. Meeting, assembly, congregation, congress; a society, company, association, club; convivial meeting, party; the abode of a respectable community; a collection, quantity, multitude, number; apparatus, equipment, equipage; — an elephant.

ety: for A. سماجة, inf. n. of سمج 'to be foul, unseemly, or ugly'
def: s.f. Deformity, unseemliness, shamefulness, turpitude; adulteration; entreaty, solicitation; adulation, flattery; — adj. Deformed, unseemly, ugly; foul, filthy, base.
s.f. Knowledge, perception, understanding, comprehension, discernment, conception, thought, mind, sense, judgment, opinion, view
subs: (kisī-ko or mujh-ko), To get sense or understanding, to attain to years of discretion; to recover (one's) senses
adj. Discerning, intelligent, sensible, wise, prudent
(-kī, or-merī, &c.), 'To come into the comprehension' (of), to be comprehended, be understood, be perceived, be apprehended.

r: samajh-būjh-kar, samajh-būjh-ke, samajh-kar, samajh-ke, samajh-būjhnā, samajhnā
ety: past conj. part. of samajh-būjhnā and samajhnā
def: adv. With full knowledge, in the exercise of the faculty of intelligence and of understanding; knowingly, wittingly; intelligently; with due thought or consideration, with discretion, discretely.

r: samajh-būjh-ke, samajh-būjh-kar, samajh-kar, samajh-ke, samajh-būjhnā, samajhnā
ety: past conj. part. of samajh-būjhnā and samajhnā
def: adv. With full knowledge, in the exercise of the faculty of intelligence and of understanding; knowingly, wittingly; intelligently; with due thought or consideration, with discretion, discretely.

r: samajh-kar, samajh-būjh-kar, samajh-būjh-ke, samajh-ke, samajh-būjhnā, samajhnā
ety: past conj. part. of samajh-būjhnā and samajhnā
def: adv. With full knowledge, in the exercise of the faculty of intelligence and of understanding; knowingly, wittingly; intelligently; with due thought or consideration, with discretion, discretely.

r: samajh-ke, samajh-būjh-kar, samajh-būjh-ke, samajh-kar, samajh-būjhnā, samajhnā
ety: past conj. part. of samajh-būjhnā and samajhnā
def: adv. With full knowledge, in the exercise of the faculty of intelligence and of understanding; knowingly, wittingly; intelligently; with due thought or consideration, with discretion, discretely.

ety: samujh˚ or samajh˚ = Prk. संवुजझ(इ) = S. सम्बुध्य(ते), rt. सम्+बुध्
def: v.t. & v.n. To perceive, know, understand, comprehend, apprehend; to learn; — to think, consider, conceive, suppose, deem, imagine, fancy; to think highly of, to consider as good or excellent or remarkable; — to come to an understanding (with, -se), to look (to one, for explanation, or payment, &c.), to settle accounts (with, either fig. or lit.), to be even (with)
subs: (-ko), and samajh-lenā, v.t. intens. of and = samajhnā.

def: adj. Intelligent, discerning, sensible, wise, prudent, considerate.
s.m. Musicians that attend dancers.
adj. & s.m. Belonging to a society or community, held in common; — a member of an assembly or congregation; a spectator.
adj. & s.m. Relating or belonging to an assembly or multitude, social; — an assistant or spectator at an assembly or meeting, member of an assembly.
s.f. The belonging to an assembly or society.
adj. & s.m. Belonging to the Sāma-veda; — one who chants the Sāma-veda.
s.m. A kind of edible grain, Panicum frumentaceum ( = sāṅwā); a kind of grain-bearing grass (found in khādar lands).
s.m. A fish on which the earth is supposed to rest; a fish (in general); the sign Pisces of the Zodiac
subs: From the earth-supporting fish (or from the bottom of the abyss) to the pinnacle of heaven.

def: adj. & s.m. Having eyes; — one who has eyes to see with.
s.f. Report, fame, reputation, celebrity, notoriety; name, appellation.
part. adj. Declared proclaimed; famed, celebrated, well-known, public, notorious.
